title: Pentanema × mucheri
intro: Pentanema × mucheri is a hybrid plant species in the daisy family, known for its vibrant purple flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pentanema × mucheri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Pentanema × mucheri is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Pentanema, which includes various daisy species native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Pentanema × mucheri is within the family Asteraceae and the order Asterales. This hybrid plant is thought to be a cross between two Pentanema species. However, its classification may require further research for accuracy.

parts: Pentanema × mucheri is known to be a hybrid of Pentanema eugeniae and Pentanema hyssopifolium.
